summary: Tested Password Reset feature (P2). Backend: 11/11 tests passed (100%). Frontend: UI flows work correctly but error handling has a pre-existing bug showing 'body stream already read' instead of proper API error messages.

minor.issue: Returns 500 for non-verified Resend emails (expected behavior in test mode - only 3dotsconnected@gmail.com works)

ui_bugs.issue: When API returns error (4xx/5xx), error message shows 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' instead of actual API error message. Affects login, password reset confirm, and likely other endpoints.
ui_bugs.root_cause: Response body is being consumed before the error handling code can read it. This is a pre-existing bug, not introduced by password reset feature.

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/password-reset/request - unverified email returns 500 (Resend test mode)
Backend: POST /api/auth/password-reset/confirm - valid code resets password (200)
Backend: Full flow with DB code - reset and login with new password
Backend: Full flow with verified email - reset and login with new password
Frontend: Login page shows 'Olvidaste tu contraseña?' link
Frontend: Clicking link shows reset-request view with email input
Frontend: After submitting email, shows code input + new password view
Frontend: Login with correct credentials works
action_items: FIX (HIGH): Error handling bug - 'body stream already read' error. The response body is being consumed before error handling code can read it. This affects all API error responses. Investigate if there's a global fetch interceptor, service worker, or browser extension causing this. The bug exists in useTenantStore.js login function and Login.jsx handleResetConfirm function.
NOTE: Resend API is in test mode - only sends emails to 3dotsconnected@gmail.com. Other emails will return 500 from the password reset request endpoint.
critical_code_review_comments: Pre-existing bug: Response body stream is being consumed before error handling code runs. This causes 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' error instead of showing actual API error messages.
The password reset feature implementation is correct - the bug is in the existing error handling pattern used throughout the app.

success_rate.frontend: Partial - UI flows work, but error handling is broken (pre-existing bug)

test_credentials.resend_verified: 3dotsconnected@gmail.com / nuevapass (only email that works with Resend test mode)

seed_data_creation: Test users created during backend tests were cleaned up. Password reset codes are stored in db.password_resets collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Password reset backend is fully working. Frontend UI renders correctly but has a pre-existing error handling bug that shows 'body stream already read' instead of actual API error messages. This bug affects all API error responses, not just password reset. The main agent needs to investigate why the response body is being consumed before the error handling code can read it.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Frontend shows 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' instead of actual API error messages
reproduction_steps: 4. Observe error message shows 'body stream already read' instead of 'Credenciales inválidas'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The Response body stream is being consumed before the error handling code can read it. This could be caused by: (1) A global fetch interceptor, (2) A service worker, (3) Browser DevTools network logging, (4) Cloudflare/ingress layer consuming the response, or (5) React StrictMode causing double-rendering (tested - not the cause).
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Need to investigate what is consuming the response body. Possible fixes: (1) Clone the response before reading, (2) Use a different approach to read the response, (3) Check for global interceptors or middleware.
